mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strong labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a certain threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy that increases borrowing costs and dampens economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently stays above 4.0% but S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and low unemployment indicates robust consumer demand and confidence, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and low UNRATE conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or below market average over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial expansion, increasing industrial production reflects strong manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This effect is amplified when inflation is contained.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month avg growth > 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 2.5%
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent growth in a low inflation environment, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts signals a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand, which can precede a slowdown in corporate earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls by > 10% from prior 3-month average
failure_condition: If HOUST declines sharply as specified, but S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive or quickly recover in over 40% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and can trigger tighter monetary policy, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as real earnings decline.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently shows high growth but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates a deteriorating labor market, which reduces consumer confidence and spending, ultimately hurting corporate profits and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.2 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises significantly as specified, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive or flat in over 30% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth signifies a robust overall economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 consistently shows strong growth but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat over any 5-year rolling window.
